claim_level_note: QEMU user-mode TCG is a functional ISA model, NOT cycle-accurate RTL. Per docs/benchmarks/claim-ladder.md this supports functional correctness and a dynamic instruction count ONLY. It does NOT support CoreMark/MHz, CPI, IPC, frequency, power, or any hardware performance claim. 'Iterations/Sec' below is QEMU host wall-clock and is explicitly NOT a CoreMark score for this core.

coremark.iterations_note: CoreMark posix port auto-scaled the run to 6000 iterations (>10s on the QEMU host); CRC self-validation passed.

next_step_for_phone_claim: Run the benchmark on L5/L6 phone or prototype silicon with real target metadata, calibration, and raw-output hashes. The CVA6 Verilator result supports only L1_RTL_FULL_SOC.
note: E1's little core (e1-pro) IS the CVA6 RV64GC core (docs/evidence/cpu_ap/core-selection.json). This is a FUNCTIONAL software-model run for an instruction-count anchor only; the cycle-accurate CVA6 Verilator number is now recorded in cva6-coremark-verilator.json.

tools.gcc: riscv64-buildroot-linux-gnu-gcc 13.3.0 (external/buildroot-2024.11/output/host/bin) — self-contained static rv64gc linux toolchain; chosen over the xpack 15.2.0 bare-metal newlib gcc because QEMU user-mode requires a Linux-syscall libc, while xpack newlib targets HTIF semihosting used by the (blocked) Verilator path
